content:
# Mark Chapter 7 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), folios 35v–36r (PDF pages 70–71) | **Source Image:** `source-texts/greek-NT/MRK_007_page_001.jpg`, `source-texts/greek-NT/MRK_007_page_002.jpg`
**Secondary Witness:** Codex Vaticanus (B, 03) — consulted via [DigiVatLib](https://digi.vatlib.it/view/MSS_Vat.gr.1209)

---

¹ And the Pharisees and some of the scribes, having-come from Jerusalem, gather-together to him.

² And having-seen that some of his disciples eat the loaves with defiled — that is, unwashed — hands...

³ For the Pharisees and all the Jews, unless they-wash the hands with-a-fist, do not eat, holding the tradition of-the elders;

⁴ and [coming] from [the] marketplace, unless they-wash, they-do not eat; and many other-[things] there-are which they-received to-hold — washings of-cups and pitchers and copper-vessels.

⁵ And the Pharisees and the scribes ask him, "Why do your disciples not walk according-to the tradition of-the elders, but eat the bread with defiled hands?"

⁶ And he said to-them, "Well did Isaiah prophesy concerning you hypocrites, as it-has-been-written: 'This people honors me with-the lips, but their heart is far-distant from me.

⁷ And in-vain they-worship me, teaching [as] teachings the commandments of-men.'

⁸ Having-left the commandment of God, you-hold the tradition of men."

⁹ And he-was-saying to-them, "Well do-you-set-aside the commandment of God, in-order-that you-may-establish your tradition.

¹⁰ For Moses said, 'Honor your father and your mother,' and, 'The-one speaking-evil-of father or mother, let-him-die [by] death.'

¹¹ But you say, 'If a-man says to-[his] father or to-[his] mother, Corban — that is, a-gift — [is] whatever from-me you-might-be-benefited,'

¹² you-no-longer allow him to-do anything for the father or the mother,

¹³ making-void the word of God by your tradition which you-handed-down. And many such-like-[things] you-do."

¹⁴ And having-called the crowd again, he-was-saying to-them, "Hear me, all, and understand:

¹⁵ There-is nothing from-outside the man, entering into him, which is-able to-defile him; but the-[things] from the man going-out are the-[things] defiling the man."

¹⁶ [If anyone has ears to hear, let him hear.]

¹⁷ And when he-entered into [a] house from the crowd, his disciples were-asking him [about] the parable.

¹⁸ And he-says to-them, "So are you also without-understanding? Do-you not perceive that everything from-outside entering into the man is not able to-defile him,

¹⁹ because it-does not enter into his heart but into the belly, and goes-out into the latrine?" — purifying all the foods.

²⁰ And he-was-saying, "The-[thing] from the man going-out, that defiles the man.

²¹ For from-within, out-of the heart of men, the evil reasonings go-out: sexual-immoralities, thefts, murders,

²² adulteries, covetousnesses, wickednesses, deceit, sensuality, an-evil eye, blasphemy, arrogance, foolishness.

²³ All these evil-[things] from-within go-out and defile the man."

²⁴ And from-there having-risen, he-went-away into the regions of Tyre. And having-entered into a-house, he-was-wanting no-one to-know, and he-was not able to-escape-notice.

²⁵ But immediately, a-woman having-heard about him, whose little-daughter had an-unclean spirit, having-come, fell at his feet.

²⁶ And the woman was a-Greek, a-Syrophoenician by-race. And she-was-asking him that he-cast-out the demon from her daughter.

²⁷ And he-was-saying to-her, "Let first the children be-satisfied, for it-is not good to-take the bread of-the children and to-throw [it] to-the little-dogs."

²⁸ But she answered and says to-him, "Lord, even the little-dogs under the table eat from the crumbs of-the children."

²⁹ And he-said to-her, "On-account-of this word, go — the demon has-gone-out from your daughter."

³⁰ And having-gone-away to her house, she-found the child having-been-laid upon the bed and the demon having-gone-out.

³¹ And having-gone-out again from the regions of Tyre, he-came through Sidon to the Sea of Galilee, through the middle of-the regions of-the Decapolis.

³² And they-bring to-him a-deaf-[man] and hardly-speaking, and they-implore him that he-may-lay the hand on-him.

³³ And having-taken him away from the crowd privately, he-put his fingers into his ears and having-spit, he-touched his tongue.

³⁴ And having-looked-up to heaven, he-groaned and says to-him, "Ephphatha," that is, "Be-opened."

³⁵ And his ears were-opened, and immediately the bond of his tongue was-loosed, and he-was-speaking correctly.

³⁶ And he-ordered them that they-should-tell no-one. But as-much-as he was-ordering them, they more-abundantly were-proclaiming [it].

³⁷ And they-were being beyond-measure astonished, saying, "He-has-done all-[things] well; both the deaf he-makes to-hear and the mute to-speak."

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **7:3-4 — Parenthetical explanation:** Mark explains Jewish purity customs for his non-Jewish audience, including washing "with a fist" (ΠΥΓΜΗ) — likely meaning up to the wrist or with a clenched fist to rub the hands. This confirms Mark's audience is predominantly Gentile.

2. **7:16 — Verse existence:** Many manuscripts include v. 16 ("If anyone has ears to hear, let him hear"). Sinaiticus appears to include it, though this verse is absent from some early witnesses and is bracketed in critical editions. Vaticanus omits it.

3. **7:19 — "purifying all foods" (ΚΑΘΑΡΙΖΩΝ ΠΑΝΤΑ ΤΑ ΒΡΩΜΑΤΑ):** The participle ΚΑΘΑΡΙΖΩΝ is masculine, which should modify "he" (Jesus), not "latrine." Mark's editorial note — "thus he declared all foods clean" — is one of the most significant editorial comments in the Gospel.

4. **7:27 — "little dogs" (ΚΥΝΑΡΙΟΙΣ):** The diminutive form — "little dogs" or "puppies" — is used, not the harsher κύνες ("dogs"). Whether this softens the statement or is simply colloquial is debated.

5. **7:34 — "Ephphatha" (ΕΦΦΑΘΑ):** Aramaic אֶתְפַּתַח (ʾeṯpattaḥ) — "be opened." Like "Talitha koum," this preserves Jesus's actual Aramaic speech.

6. **7:31 — Geographical route:** Jesus goes from Tyre through Sidon to the Sea of Galilee via the Decapolis — a circuitous route through Gentile territory. This geographical note underscores the Gentile mission theme of this section.

— EMET, AI Translator
